name: Robinhood Crypto
summary: Official product surface for Robinhood Chain — the public face of the L2 and onchain crypto products.
about: Robinhood Chain is pitched as a permissionless Layer 2 for financial services and real-world assets, developed on Arbitrum infrastructure.|The brokerage app and the chain are different products: app = accounts, KYC, eligibility; chain = rails anyone can (in principle) deploy on.|Use official pages and docs as primary sources before secondary CT maps.
related: Robinhood Wallet,Robinhood Earn,Stock Tokens (official),Robinhood Chain Docs
risks: Official narrative ≠ security review of every third-party app on the chain.

name: Robinhood Chain Docs
summary: Primary developer documentation for building on Robinhood Chain.
about: Start here for network parameters, tooling, and builder-facing claims.|Prefer docs over CT threads when the two disagree.
risks: Docs lag product launches; always check last-updated dates.

name: Robinhood Wallet
notes: Self-custody wallet surface; trending tokens; Pay checkout claims
summary: Self-custody wallet surface that points users at Robinhood Chain activity (swaps, trending, checkout UX).
about: Wallet features (trending tokens, Apple/Google Pay checkout narratives, fee/gas promos) are product UX on top of the chain.|Time-box every promo claim — gas waivers and fee holidays expire.|Wallet inclusion of a token is not an endorsement of safety or liquidity.
risks: Promo windows and trending lists change; verify terms and dates.

name: Robinhood Earn
notes: USDG lending via Morpho; ~7% APY narrative; insurance claims in newsroom
summary: App-facing USDG lending product; underlying rails cited as Morpho with partner stack support.
about: Earn lets eligible users lend dollar-backed USDG via a self-custody path, with an official ~7% APY narrative and insurance claims in newsroom copy.|Infrastructure is powered by Morpho; partners named in launch narrative include Steakhouse, Ethena, Spark, and Maple.|This is an app product experience sitting on chain lending rails — not a guarantee of yield.
risks: Yield is variable; eligibility and insurance terms are jurisdiction- and product-specific. NFA.

name: Stock Tokens (official)
summary: Official tokenized equities product on Robinhood Chain — the RWA thesis surface, not a memecoin market.
about: Stock Tokens are pitched as tokenized exposure to named equities/ETFs for eligible users outside restricted jurisdictions.|Official pages note they are not available in the US and are subject to restrictions elsewhere.|Listed onchain does not mean deep two-sided liquidity — listed ≠ liquid.|Use for product/mechanism understanding, not price targets.
risks: Jurisdiction limits; brochure listings can fail at size; not direct share ownership in all legal senses — read official terms.

name: Vlad Tenev
summary: CEO / co-founder signal account for product direction (e.g. builder calls to embed stock tokens).
about: Useful as Tier-0 primary signal for strategy, not as a protocol docs substitute.|Landscape role: Company leadership signal. Official Robinhood product or leadership surface for the chain.|Hoodscape is orientation only — verify primary docs, contracts, and eligibility before interacting. NFA.
risks: Social posts are not legal or product documentation.

name: USDG / Paxos
notes: Dollar-backed stable narrative; dominant stables mcap on chain
summary: Dollar-backed stable narrative on Robinhood Chain; dominant share of stables mcap in early metrics.
about: USDG is central to Earn and settlement stories on RH Chain.|Stable dominance on a young chain can reflect product design and incentives, not permanent equilibrium.|Always separate stable float from DEX meme volume when reading landscape metrics.
risks: Issuer, attestation, and redeemability details belong in primary issuer docs.

name: Arbitrum
notes: Orbit stack parent for RH Chain
summary: Orbit / L2 stack parent for Robinhood Chain — security and architecture context.
about: RH Chain is developed on Arbitrum’s Layer 2 infrastructure with high-throughput / ~100ms block claims in official copy.|“Built on Arbitrum” is a stack claim, not a full safety sticker for every app, bridge, or sequencer operational choice.|Read Orbit/L2 docs for inheritance model, then map residual risks separately.
risks: Stack inheritance ≠ app-level bridge/ops risk.

risks: Wrong feed configuration is an app risk even when the oracle network is reputable.

name: Uniswap
notes: V2/V3/V4 (+ UniswapX narrative) primary public AMM
summary: Primary public AMM on Robinhood Chain (v2/v3/v4 narrative) — main open liquidity venue by volume/TVL.

name: Rialto

risks: Venue design and access may differ from permissionless public AMM UX.
